method: Multifractal Detrended Fluctuation Analysis (MFDFA) spectrum width Δh
libs_implemented: Implemented in numpy from scratch (pywt/statsmodels missing): MFDFA per Kantelhardt 2002 — profile cumsum, vectorized DFA-1 segment detrend (forward+backward), q-order fluctuation function F_q(s) incl. q->0 log limit, generalized Hurst h(q)=slope(logF_q vs log s), multifractal spectrum width Δh=h(q_lo)-h(q_hi). Causal rolling-window wrapper + numerical prefix-recompute causality proof. Backtest/PSR/DSR from engine.
notes: MFDFA implemented from scratch in numpy (Kantelhardt 2002): profile cumsum, DFA-1 segment detrend (forward+backward), q-order F_q(s), h(q)=slope(logF_q vs log s), spectrum width Δh=h(q_lo)-h(q_hi). SANITY: white noise Δh=+0.05~0 (monofractal), binomial cascade Δh=+0.85>>0 (multifractal) -> implementation validated. Rolling Δh is a SLOW regime feature (trailing 252-bar ~84d window, recomputed every 4 bars, ffill); proved causal (Δh from trailing window == from truncated prefix, max|Δ|=0e+00). STANDALONE Δh-conditioned momentum/reversal: IS-pick win=252 q=(-3,+3) lb=12 mode=trend_calm, OOS Sharpe=0.33 ret=11.6% PSR=0.669, FAILS within-family DSR SR*=0.0593 over 216 variants. OVERLAY (Δh gates base book): IS-pick base=btc feat=level dir=derisk_calm; base OOS Sharpe=0.10 maxDD=-54.9% -> gated Sharpe=0.42 maxDD=-13.5% (PSR=0.714). Multifractal spectrum width is a real, validated regime statistic but as a tradeable signal here it does not survive cost+deflation; verdict=DEAD, role=none. Slow feature: low turnover but coarse timing.
data_caveats: close-to-close 8h perp closes; maxDD is close-to-close (no intra-bar liquidation/gap modeling -> optimistic). Δh is a slow ~84-day-window statistic recomputed every 4 bars and forward-filled, so it gives COARSE timing; standalone book is directional (long+short per-coin), NOT market-neutral. q=+-5 with L=252 has known finite-size bias in h(q) magnitude but the WIDTH/regime DYNAMICS (what we trade) are the relevant signal. Many knobs => heavily deflated.
